title: Encholirium oreadicum
taxonomy: Encholirium oreadicum is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. This plant is native to Brazil and is part of the Encholirium genus, which is a group of terrestrial bromeliads. Encholirium oreadicum is classified as an epiphyte, meaning it grows on other plants or surfaces. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and the clade Angiosperms. The plant's classification is further broken down into the phylum Magnoliophyta and the class Liliopsida.

intro: Encholirium oreadicum, also known as "mossy air plant," is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it a potential treatment for wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Encholirium oreadicum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.
